full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic main wing spar for a radio-controlled design/build/fly competition aircraft. The aircraft is limited to a maximum wingspan of 5 ft (1524 mm); the scoring rule multiplies empty weight by wingspan (rated aircraft cost). The wing is a tapered rectangular planform with root chord 350 mm, tip chord 250 mm, span 1524 mm (5 ft), dihedral 3 deg. The main spar is a single-piece I-beam running from wingtip to wingtip through the centerline; cross section 30 mm wide flanges x 3 mm flange thickness, web height 25 mm x 2 mm web thickness, uniform over the full span. Material: carbon/epoxy unidirectional prepreg (E = 135 GPa longitudinal, 10 GPa transverse, density 1600 kg/m^3, longitudinal tensile/compressive strength 1500 MPa, transverse 50 MPa). Mission-level loads: maximum take-off weight (MTOW) = 6.5 kg (aircraft + 4.0 kg payload); design limit load factor n_z = 4.0 g (standard structural demonstration/wing tip test). Primary load cases: (R1) 4 g symmetric pull-up with full payload: distributed lift equal to MTOW x 4 g = 255 N applied as elliptical spanwise load giving a root bending moment M_root = 0.5 * 255 * 0.4 * 0.762 = 39 N*m (approx); max fiber stress in spar flanges <= 1500 MPa / 3.0 = 500 MPa. (R2) Wing-tip structural demonstration: aircraft is held at the wingtips and shall support 2.5 x MTOW = 16.25 kg hanging from the fuselage without structural failure or permanent deformation; equivalent tip-load spar bending check: max von Mises <= 500 MPa. (R3) Takeoff distance: ground roll must not exceed 100 ft (30.5 m) with MTOW (non-structural, included for completeness). (R4) Spar mass <= 180 g. (R5) First wing bending natural frequency >= 25 Hz (hard-mounted at centerline) to avoid coupling with propeller 1P at typical motor RPM 4000 (66 Hz) within a 2x margin. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static for R1, R2) + modal (R5). Thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling explicitly NOT required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 spar, laminate schedule, and a report listing spar mass in g, max fiber stress per case in MPa, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic carbon/epoxy main wing spar for a 5 ft-span radio-controlled design-build-fly competition aircraft (scoring multiplies empty weight by wingspan). The wing is a tapered rectangular planform (root chord 350 mm, tip chord 250 mm, span 1524 mm, 3 deg dihedral); the spar is a one-piece uniform-section I-beam running wingtip to wingtip (30 mm flanges x 3 mm flange thickness, 25 mm web x 2 mm web). MTOW 6.5 kg (aircraft + 4.0 kg payload); design limit load factor n_z = 4.0.

Load cases.
- LC1 4 g symmetric pull-up: elliptical spanwise lift of MTOW x 4 g = 255 N, giving a ~39 N*m root bending moment.
- LC2 wingtip structural demonstration: aircraft supported at the wingtips while 2.5 x MTOW (16.25 kg) hangs from the fuselage.
- LC3 takeoff: ground roll <= 100 ft (30.5 m) at MTOW (context, non-structural).
- LC4 modal: hard-mounted at centerline, first wing-bending mode must clear propeller 1P (~66 Hz at 4000 RPM).

prompt.title: Monolithic carbon spar for 5 ft design/build/fly UAV
prompt.brief: Full-span 1524 mm UD-carbon I-beam spar carrying 4 g pull-up with 6.5 kg MTOW and the wingtip-hold demonstration (2.5x MTOW).

load_cases.name: wingtip_hold_2.5x
load_cases.description: aircraft held at wingtips, fuselage hanging

boundary_conditions.mounting: spar centerline cantilevered at fuselage for structural; centerline hard-mounted for modal
boundary_conditions.loads: elliptical spanwise lift / concentrated tip hold

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero reduced to statically-equivalent lift distribution.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: I-beam spar bending dominated; no compressive buckling driver.

eval_coverage: structural demonstration test translation to FEA
